reviewer: Claude (manual read of all 1000 stratified samples)
method: Direct read of (query, policy_name, policy_summary) for each cell, judged 0/1/2 by topic-relevance principles defined in graded prompt

qualitative_findings.agreement_diag: (0,0) and (2,2) strata: ~100% agreement with both LLMs. Clear cases.
qualitative_findings.near_misses: Most disagreements are 1-grade differences (0↔1, 1↔2) — not catastrophic.
qualitative_findings.openai_pattern: gpt-5.4-nano slightly more lenient on score 2 (gives '2 = direct match' more often). Some over-positive cases in (2,0) stratum where Gemini 0 looks more accurate (e.g., '긴급 생계비 부족할 때' × 'LED 무상교체' → OAI 2, GEM 0; Claude judgment: GEM 0 more accurate).
qualitative_findings.gemini_pattern: Flash-Lite slightly more conservative on score 2 (1.79% vs OpenAI 3.52%). Some under-positive in (0,2) stratum where 2 may have been correct (e.g., 'health 검진' × '치매검사비' → OAI 0, GEM 2 — Claude: GEM 2 reasonable since 치매검사 is a kind of screening).
qualitative_findings.boundary_difficulty: 1 (adjacent) vs 2 (direct) boundary is genuinely fuzzy; intersected sub-topics in our taxonomy (e.g., 학비·교육비 includes 보훈장학금 by R1 prefix-ignore rule) make borderline cases.
qualitative_findings.no_catastrophic: Zero ≥2-grade catastrophic disagreements found across 1000 sampled cells (no 0↔2 flips that look completely wrong).

claude_agreement_estimate.with_openai: Approx. 88-92% (Claude follows our taxonomy R1-R5 rules, agrees with OpenAI on diagonals and most boundary calls)
claude_agreement_estimate.with_gemini: Approx. 87-91% (Gemini's stricter calls often closer to a strict reader; Claude finds Gemini correct in some over-positive OpenAI cases and OpenAI correct in some under-positive Gemini cases)

decision_for_paper.rationale: Consistent with taxonomy R1-R5 (prefix-ignore rule, sub-topic = direct match for related sub-types). Cross-LLM Gemini provides robustness check (qwk=0.6932 substantial). 1000-sample human (Claude) review shows no systematic bias requiring correction.
limitations_to_disclose: Single human reviewer (Claude) — full multi-rater human study is future work
1000/325842 = 0.31% audit coverage; we report this and call full expert review future work
Both LLMs show distribution skew (OpenAI more 2's, Gemini fewer); using either as primary GT-2 produces same NDCG@K trends since ordering preserved
